domain: Andean chuño preservation (cyclic alternation of freeze + trample + thaw; phase changes plus mechanical pressure produce stable dry product)
specific_mechanism: N-cycle 3-phase alternation: phase A captures targets, phase B applies a destructive pressure transformation, phase C relaxes; repeated yields a much-compressed stable end state.
llm_application: FTTC (Freeze-Trample-Thaw Compression): an LLM quantization schedule that repeats N×{FREEZE-snapshot weights + identify magnitude-outliers (analog ice-crystals); TRAMPLE-distill outlier energy back into bulk via gradient pressure; THAW-relax quantization grid to next bit-width}. Distinct from one-shot PTQ and standard QAT in that mass-redistribution and grid-relaxation alternate over multiple chuño-style cycles.
novelty_claim: AQLM (2401.06118), GPTVQ, PV-Tuning, ARB-LLM all do interleaved quantization but the explicit 3-phase {snapshot outliers / pressure-redistribute via distillation / grid-relax} cycle structured after a multi-night preservation process is not yet published — the closest is ARB-LLM (alternating refined binarizations) which alternates differently.
